channel.title: Meta on Pieter Belmans
channel.description: Recent content in Meta on Pieter Belmans

item.title: Moving from Jekyll to Hugo
item.description:
Almost ten years ago, I moved from WordPress to Jekyll. It felt like time for another change, so I have now moved the site to Hugo. There was no particular need for it, build times weren't too bad, but it is nice to see how much faster Hugo is in building my website.

The migration is complete, and I have tried to make sure everything still works as intended. Let me know if something did break!

item.title: I've joined the editorial board of Journal of Noncommutative Geometry
item.description:
I am pleased and honoured to announce that I have joined the editorial board of Journal of Noncommutative Geometry. The word algebraic is implicit in its name, as the journal serves both the NCG and NCAG communities.

Please submit your interesting and high-quality papers on noncommutative algebraic geometry, so that the journal can continue its role as a leading journal in this particular niche!

item.title: Updated contact information
item.description:
Today is my first day (physically) at Utrecht University.

From now on, please get in touch using p.belmans@uu.nl. My former University of Luxembourg address is no longer active: it is their policy to not have a grace period or forwarding set up, which is pretty inconvenient for any academic. I am sorry for the inconvenience this will cause...

If you find any out-of-date contact information, please let me know!

item.title: Moving to Utrecht University
item.description:
I am really excited to share here the news that I will be moving to Utrecht University, starting from January 1. I am also very much looking forward to integrating in the vibrant Dutch algebraic geometry community, which I participated in as an outsider over the past 10+ years. I will at the same time miss working in Luxembourg.

Today it was also announced that I am the recipient of a Vidi grant, from the NWO, for a project focusing on noncommutative algebraic geometry. I thank the NWO for the confidence they have in me, and I'm looking forward to all of the exciting noncommutative algebraic geometry I will be working on!

item.title: I've joined the editorial board of Experimental Mathematics
item.description:
This has been on the website for a few months now, but I have joined the editorial board for Experimental Mathematics, following the "reboot" led by Al Kasprzyk.

Please submit your interesting and high-quality experimental papers, so that the journal can continue its role as a leading journal in this particular niche!

item.title: Starting at the University of Luxembourg
item.description: Monday was my first day at the University of Luxembourg. This means a new email address to reach me (pieter.belmans@uni.lu).

item.title: Moving to the University of Luxembourg
item.description:
I am very excited to share the news that I'll be starting as an assistant professor at the University of Luxembourg this summer. I'm really looking forward to start!

Not entirely unrelated to the message above the fold, I'd like to advertise:

  • this postdoc position, associated to Sarah Scherotzke.
  • these 3 PhD positions, which can be taken up with 5 of my future colleagues.

Especially for the postdoc position: in case you wish to work on topics that Sarah Scherotzke is interested in, it could be useful to know that I'll be starting there too, given the overlap between our research interests.

item.title: Moving to the University of Bonn
item.description:
A quick update: starting April 1, I will move to the University of Bonn. You can already start emailing me at pbelmans@math.uni-bonn.de.

Also, you might have noticed a lack of blog posts in the past weeks, which can be explained by me being on vacation.

item.title: Starting my postdoc
item.description: A quick update: This week I have started my postdoc at the Max Planck Institute for Mathematics in Bonn. Feel free to come and say hi in office 402.

item.title: Moving from WordPress to Jekyll
item.description: My first post was little over six years ago, and it has become time to move my weblog from WordPress to Jekyll, integrating it with my personal webpage. I have become a great fan of Jekyll, and after so many years I started to dislike the fact that I had two completely disjoint websites: one for blogging, and the other acting more like my business card. This is no longer the case now.

item.title: Announcing ncag.info
item.description:
A while ago I decided to create a website dedicated to noncommutative algebraic geometry. It has been (silently) available for a while now, but I guess it's time to realise it to the public. You can find it at ncag.info.

So far it is nothing more than a list of conferences that might be interesting for noncommutative algebraic geometers. I've been keeping track of these since the beginning of 2016, and I will continue to do so. If I have missed an event that would fit on this list, please tell me. If you have any other suggestions, please say so.

item.title: Announcing a seminar
item.description:
To make sense of the next post, and potentially attract more attendees, I want to announce a student seminar on algebra and algebraic geometry at the University of Antwerp. It's called ANAGRAMS, which is an acronym for "Antwerp algebraic geometry, rings and more seminar".

All the interesting information can be found on the website, to summarise: PhD students give lectures for PhD students on subjects related to algebraic geometry and algebra (based on the research areas we are active in). We pick a subject, work on it for a few weeks, then take another one. It's about learning things, it's not a research seminar.

item.title: Personal update
item.description:
Not that this blog is considered to be the medium for all my personal tidbits, but to sketch a little context for what I might be blogging about in the upcoming year I think it is appropriate to explain my current whereabouts and plans.

I will be doing the second year of the master in mathematics at Ecole Polytechnique and Université Paris-Sud XI, specializing in algebraic geometry and number theory. So this means my blog might get a little more focus where mathematics is concerned. And probably I will be creating all kinds of LaTeX templates tailored to my current home universities :).

item.title: Lessons learned from blogging
item.description:
Some people may have realized it by looking at their aggregators and more specifically the date I posted on. I followed a "one post in three days" schedule for December. I wrote some short snippets in between but all my "real" posts were scheduled 3 days from each other. This way I could build up a backlog of material when I felt inspired :).

Some conclusions:

  • there are many things to blog about;
  • it is amazing how fast a post gets body, i.e. gets long enough to be more than a side note;
  • scheduling posts is the best invention in WordPress ever;
  • I actually use my own posts on LaTeX when I need the topic I wrote about, they provide a good overview of what I deemed useful about that little part of LaTeX :-).

But for now, my publishing rate will diminish greatly: I'm studying for my exams and I've got more of them than I want. But as TeX Hacks is on a posting spree with 4 interesting posts on 5 days I'd suggest you all move there for one month. And I'd like to thank TeXample.net for adding me to their aggregator, I can now reach a much broader audience.

item.title: Antwerp ACM Student Chapter and Te(ch/X)Talks
item.description: At the University of Antwerp we (a group of 4 students) started a Student Chapter, in order to facilitate computer science-related activities. And last week we had our first TechTalk, in which I gave a little TeXTalk. I gave a quick overview of TeX's and LaTeX's history and tried to make clear the distinction between stuff like pdftex and the pdfLaTeX macro set. For archiving purposes it (and all the other talks) is listed at the official website which is due to move in a few days or weeks to the ACM webspace so expect this link to go down (unless I fix it when due).

item.title: Introduction
item.description:
Not that I'm intending to blog regularly, but at least there is a completely set-up space to do so in case the urge kicks in. When the actual blogging will start, it will be featuring stuff on (computational or algorithmic) composition and ramblings on both computer science and mathematics.

For now, please enjoy yourself reading something more interesting. I'd suggest Pafko at the Wall from Don DeLillo.
